zh.lifeSummary: Lady Matilda de Byron是Robert de Byron of Melling Manor和wife Robert de Byron的女兒，於大約1293年出生在英國爾金斯利。她於大約1312年與Sir William Gerard III在英國英格蘭爾金斯利結婚。他們至少是3個兒子的父母。她去世於1361年4月28日之後在英國英格蘭爾金斯利。

zh-hans.lifeSummary: Lady Matilda de Byron是Robert de Byron of Melling Manor和wife Robert de Byron的女儿，于大约1293年出生在英国爾金斯利。她于大约1312年与Sir William Gerard III在英国英格兰爾金斯利结婚。他们至少是3个儿子的父母。她去世于1361年4月28日之后在英国英格兰爾金斯利。

nameMeanings.name: de
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Indian Dilip, Anirban, Gopal, Alok, Anindya, Arunava, Asit, Asok, Dibyendu, Goutam, Indra, Indranil.

variants.name: Dey
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Indian Ashit, Indu, Naina, Partha, Soumya, Ajit, Anjan, Anjana, Anup, Arjun, Arun, Ashim.
English, Scottish, and Welsh: variant of   Day  .
German: topographic name from a field name in North Rhine-Westphalia, denoting a sizeable piece of land, from Middle Low German dē ‘thigh’ (referring to the shape of the property).

nameMeanings.name: Byron
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: English: habitational name from Byram (Yorkshire), named with Old English bȳrum ‘at the cattle sheds’, dative plural of bȳre ‘byre’. This name has occasionally been adopted as a Jewish surname, presumably as an Americanized form of some similar (like-sounding) Jewish name.

nameMeanings.name: Matilda
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: Latinized form of a Germanic personal name derived from maht, meht ‘might’ + hild ‘battle’. This was the name of an early German queen ( 895–968 ), wife of Henry the Fowler, who was noted for her piety and generosity. It was also the name of the wife of William the Conqueror and of the daughter of Henry I of England ( see Maud ). The name was introduced into England by the Normans, and this Latinized form is the one that normally occurs in medieval records, while the vernacular form Maud was the one in everyday use. Matilda was revived in England as a learned form in the 18th century.

historicEvents.title: Famine Strikes Europe
historicEvents.description: A famine caused by cold winters and wet summers across Europe caused millions to die due to starvation. Desperation from hunger caused high levels of crime, stampedes in bread lines in London, infanticide, and even cannibalism throughout Europe.

historicEvents.title: First War of Scottish Independence
historicEvents.description: The First War of Scottish Independence began after England invaded Scotland in 1296. The Treaty of Edinburgh was signed in 1328 which declared peace between the two kingdoms and brought the war to an end.

historicEvents.title: The Hundred Years' War
historicEvents.description: The Hundred Years' War between France and England was largely due to the question of legitimate succession to the French crown.

en.lifeSummary: Lady Matilda de Byron was born about 1293, in Kingsley, Cheshire, England as the daughter of Robert de Byron of Melling Manor and wife Robert de Byron. She married Sir William Gerard III about 1312, in Kingsley, Cheshire, England, United Kingdom. They were the parents of at least 3 sons. She died after 28 April 1361, in Kingsley, Cheshire, England, United Kingdom.

fr.lifeSummary: Lady Matilda de Byron est née environ 1293, à Kingsley, Cheshire, Angleterre, fille de Robert de Byron of Melling Manor et wife Robert de Byron. Elle a épousé Sir William Gerard III environ 1312, à Kingsley, Cheshire, Angleterre, Royaume-Uni. Ils ont eu au moins 3 fils. Elle est décédée après 28 avril 1361, à Kingsley, Cheshire, Angleterre, Royaume-Uni.

de.lifeSummary: Lady Matilda de Byron wurde um 1293 in Kingsley, Cheshire, England, als Tochter von Robert de Byron of Melling Manor und wife Robert de Byron geboren. Sie hat um 1312 in Kingsley, Cheshire, England, United Kingdom Sir William Gerard III geheiratet. Sie hatten mindestens 3 Söhne. Sie starb nach 28. April 1361 in Kingsley, Cheshire, England, United Kingdom.

it.lifeSummary: Lady Matilda de Byron è nata il circa 1293 nel Kingsley, Cheshire, England come figlia di Robert de Byron of Melling Manor e wife Robert de Byron. Lei sposato Sir William Gerard III circa 1312, nel Kingsley, Cheshire, Inghilterra, Regno Unito. Erano genitori di almeno 3 figli. Morì dopo il 28 aprile 1361, nel Kingsley, Cheshire, Inghilterra, Regno Unito.

ja.lifeSummary: Lady Matilda de Byronは約1293年にイギリスチェシュアイアキングスリーでRobert de Byron of Melling Manorとwife Robert de Byronの娘として生まれた。約1312年にイギリスイギリスチェシュアイアキングスリーでSir William Gerard IIIと結婚した。彼らは少なくとも3人の息子の親であった。彼女は1361年4月28日以後にイギリスイギリスチェシュアイアキングスリーで死去した。

ko.lifeSummary: Lady Matilda de Byron(은)는 대략 1293년 영국 체실르 킨그슬리에서 Robert de Byron of Melling Manor(와)과 wife Robert de Byron의 딸로 태어났다. 그녀는 대략 1312년 영국 잉글랜드 체실르 킨그슬리에서 Sir William Gerard III(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 세 명의 아들을 두었다. 그녀는 1361년 4월 28일 이후 영국 잉글랜드 체실르 킨그슬리에서 사망했다.

pt.lifeSummary: Lady Matilda de Byron nasceu aproximadamente 1293, em Kingsley, Cheshire, Inglaterra filha de Robert de Byron of Melling Manor e wife Robert de Byron. Ela casou-se com Sir William Gerard III aproximadamente 1312, em Kingsley, Cheshire, Inglaterra, United Kingdom. Eles tiveram pelo menos 3 filhos. Ela faleceu após 28 de abril de 1361, em Kingsley, Cheshire, Inglaterra, United Kingdom.

ru.lifeSummary: Lady Matilda de Byron родилась около 1293 г. в местности кингсли, чешир, Англия. Её родителей звали Robert de Byron of Melling Manor и wife Robert de Byron. Она вышла замуж за человека по имени Sir William Gerard III около 1312 г. в местности кингсли, чешир, Англия, Великобритания. У них было по крайней мере 3 сына. Она умерла после 28 апрель 1361 г. в местности кингсли, чешир, Англия, Великобритания.

es.lifeSummary: Lady Matilda de Byron nació aproximadamente 1293, en Kingsley, Cheshire, Inglaterra como hija de Robert de Byron of Melling Manor y wife Robert de Byron. Se casó con Sir William Gerard III aproximadamente 1312, en Kingsley, Cheshire, Inglaterra, Reino Unido. Fueron padres de por lo menos 3 hijos. Murió después de 28 de abril de 1361, en Kingsley, Cheshire, Inglaterra, Reino Unido.
